---
title: "Data-Driven Fleet Operations: Demand Analytics and Smart Charging for Urban Ride-Hailing in Chicago" # Give your report a title
course: "Advanced Analytics and Applications" # Specify the course name
team: "06" # Specify your team number
author:
  - name: Louis Schreier
    student-id: "7381065"
  - name: Daniel Schumacher
    student-id: "7409287"
  - name: Timo Ercole
    student-id: "7408459"
contact: student@smail.uni-koeln.de # Specify one contact for the project
supervisor: "Univ.-Prof. Dr. Wolfgang Ketter"
co-supervisor: "Janik Muires" # TA of the course is the co-supervisor
date: 2026-07-26 # Date of submission
---

<!-- While you could use \clearpage or \newpage, this is the recommended way to insert a page break in Quarto as it translates to the native page break command for the output format you are using (e.g., \clearpage for PDF, <div style="page-break-after: always;"></div> for HTML). -->
{{< pagebreak >}}

\pagenumbering{arabic}
\setcounter{page}{1}

{{< include sections/01-introduction.qmd >}}

{{< include sections/02-data-description.qmd >}}

{{< include sections/03-data-preparation.qmd >}}

{{< include sections/04-descriptive-analytics.qmd >}}

{{< include sections/05-analytics.qmd >}}

{{< include sections/06-conclusions.qmd >}}
